title: Returning a Social Security Payment Received After Death
summary: A payment received around the time of death sometimes needs to be returned — knowing the process avoids confusion or accidental issues later.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Returning a Social Security Payment Received After Death | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A payment received around the time of death sometimes needs to be returned — knowing the process avoids confusion or accidental issues later.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Social Security benefits cover the prior month, so timing can be confusing.
A payment covering the month of death is generally not owed to the estate.
Direct deposit reversals are usually handled by the bank, in coordination with Social Security.

paragraphs: Because Social Security payments are issued for the previous month, a payment arriving shortly after a death may actually cover a month the person was alive for, or it may need to be returned — the timing takes some untangling.
If a payment needs to be returned, this is often handled by contacting the bank (for direct deposits) or Social Security directly, who can guide the specific return process.

nextSteps: Don't spend or distribute a recent Social Security payment until eligibility is confirmed.
Contact Social Security or the bank to clarify what's owed.
Ask for written confirmation once resolved.
disclaimer: General information — Social Security can confirm the specific status of any payment in question.
